# Transcrição: Mind_&_Mouth_connection

- **Fonte:** Mind_&_Mouth_connection
- **Duração:** 01:18
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:18]

How to be more articulate, not letting your chains of thought deviate you from the topic of conversation. There is such thing I believe as a mind and mouth connection. Right now what I'm doing is I'm thinking and I'm talking at the same time, and if I don't have a good mind and mouth connection, then I'm not able to do what I'm doing now. And the mind and mouth connection requires practice. You have to learn how to speak your thoughts. I find that a lot of the times when people are thinking, they often can't talk. And often there are times when people ask you a question, point blank, and you're at work, and you have to think on the spot and speak. So what I would highly encourage you to do to practice this is it's a very simple little game you can play and go to a random word generator online, generate a random word. So all of a sudden if you see the word duck, then you just have to talk about a duck. Ducks are yellow, ducks have a beak, ducks have two eyes, ducks can swim in water. Ducks are really frightening. I remember once when I was young, my mom took me to the pond to feed some ducks and then I threw the bread at the duck, but then I ran out of bread and then the duck got really angry and it started chasing me around. One of the reasons why I hate ducks, and you just speak what comes to your mind. And I really believe that simple little exercise helps strengthen the mind and mouth kind of connection. And as you get better at that soon in the future, when you're speaking on a topic, you're able to stay on topic, but more than that, you're able to just kind of speak your thoughts.
